macro_segments.title: Gemara: Why Three Separate Cases Needed in Mishna

micro_segments.title: Question: Why Enumerate Multiple Scenarios if Principle is Same
Answer: Different Intuitions About Risk in Each Scenario
Boat Case: Leaving Cargo Seems Like Natural Opportunity for Mischief
Store Case: Fear of Discovery If Door Is Locked
Teaching Point: Fear of Surprise Discovery Sufficient Even Without Time Specification

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yochanan: Debate Limited to Plaster Seals, Not Tar

Chachamim's Response: Thief Assumes No Discovery and Plasters Invisibly
Ruling: Halacha Like Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel

macro_segments.title: Gemara: Problematic Question on Sealed Wine with Non-Jews

micro_segments.title: Lenient View: One Seal Sufficient If Owner Will Inspect Later

Minhag Ha-Olam (Custom): Single Seal Even Without Owner Recognition

Requirement: Recipient Must Recognize Vessel to Prevent Complete Substitution

macro_segments.title: Gemara: Prostitute Cases and Risk Assessment by Patron Gender

micro_segments.title: Case: Non-Jewish Prostitute with Jewish Patron at Table Eating Wine
Ruling: Wine Permitted Because Jewish Patron Controls Situation
Explanation: Patron's Yetzer for Sexual Sin Does Not Extend to Libation Sin
Reverse Case: Jewish Prostitute with Non-Jewish Patron
Ruling: Wine Forbidden Because Prostitute Follows Patron's Will
Logic: Patron's Control Determines Whether Risk of Touching or Libation Occurs
